name: Río Real y Sierra Blanca – circular desde Ojén
region_intro: Mountain biking around Ojén offers a diverse landscape for riders, situated between the Mediterranean Sea and the Sierra de las Nieves and Sierra Blanca mountain ranges. The terrain features a mix of green, western sections with roots, sandy forest floors, and compact rocks, contrasting with the dry, rocky, and loose conditions of the brown, eastern side. This region provides varied challenges, from flowy singletrack through pine forests to technical descents and significant elevation changes. Best mountain bike trails around Ojén The most popular mountain bike trail is Río Real y Sierra Blanca – circular desde Ojén, a 14.7 miles (23.6 km) trail that takes 2 hours 55 minutes to complete, offering challenging climbs through the Sierra Blanca mountains. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Ojén Caves – Cuevas de Ojén loop from centro plaza puerto banus, a difficult 21.2 miles (34.1 km) path that explores the varied terrain surrounding the village. Local mountain bikers also love the Ojén – Ojén Caves loop from Pato Viudo, a 20.4 miles (32.9 km) trail leading through diverse landscapes, often completed in about 3 hours 6 minutes. Mountain biking around Ojén is defined by the dramatic Sierra de las Nieves and Sierra Blanca mountains, rocky and sandy trails, and extensive pine forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to difficult, technical descents. The routes in Ojén are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 170 reviews. More than 1400 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Ojén's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when mountain biking around Ojén?
A: The terrain around Ojén is quite diverse. The western side features roots, sandy and soft forest floors, and compact rocks, offering a more flowy experience. In contrast, the eastern side is characterized by dry, rocky, and loose terrain with steep or technical sections. This variety ensures trails suitable for different skill levels, from challenging downhill descents to more technical climbs through the Sierra Blanca mountains.

Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in the Ojén area?
A: There are over 15 mountain bike trails around Ojén listed on komoot, ranging from moderate to difficult. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 170 reviews.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Ojén?
A: Yes, several popular routes are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e994259296/rio-real-y-sierra-blanca-circular-desde-ojen" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Real y Sierra Blanca – circular desde Ojén&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 23.6 km trail. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21818512" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ojén Caves – Cuevas de Ojén loop from centro plaza puerto banus&lt;/a&gt;, which is a difficult 34.1 km path exploring varied terrain.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Ojén?
A: Ojén benefits from a favorable climate with plenty of sun and mild temperatures, making the period from &lt;strong&gt;September to May ideal&lt;/strong&gt; for mountain biking. Even in winter, conditions are generally pleasant for riding.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the mountain bike trails in Ojén?
A: The trails often pass through the stunning Sierra de las Nieves Natural Park and Sierra Blanca mountains, offering panoramic views. You might encounter natural springs, pine forests, and even hidden caves. Notable natural attractions include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4254120" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Upper Puente Palo Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3847454" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Charco del Canalón Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/719931" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Charco de la Virgen Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/817714" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Concha Peak&lt;/a&gt; is also a prominent landmark in the region.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in Ojén?
A: While many trails in Ojén are rated moderate to difficult due to the mountainous terrain and technical sections, there are some easier, flowy trails through sandy pine forests that might be suitable for families with some mountain biking experience. For example, the guide lists one easy route and seven moderate routes that could be explored.

Q: What are some of the more challenging mountain bike routes in Ojén?
A: For experienced riders seeking a challenge, routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e994259296/rio-real-y-sierra-blanca-circular-desde-ojen" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Real y Sierra Blanca – circular desde Ojén&lt;/a&gt; offer significant elevation changes and technical sections. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33910670" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Río Molinos – Ojén Caves loop from Ojén&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 79 km route with nearly 1900m of elevation gain, providing a substantial test of endurance and skill.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Ojén?
A: The komoot community highly rates the mountain biking experience in Ojén, with an average score of 4.4 stars. Reviewers often praise the dramatic mountain scenery of the Sierra de las Nieves and Sierra Blanca, the diverse terrain offering both flowy and technical sections, and the extensive pine forests that provide a stunning backdrop for rides.

Q: Are there any viewpoints along the mountain bike trails in Ojén?
A: Yes, the region offers spectacular panoramic views. From the Natural Viewpoint of El Juanar and El Corzo in Sierra Blanca, you can enjoy lush pine forests, the sea, and the mountains. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3162044" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Gitanos Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; is another excellent spot to take in the scenery.

Q: Can I find places to eat or drink in Ojén after a ride?
A: Ojén is a picturesque village with a strong local community. You'll find various cafes and pubs within the village itself, making it a convenient starting or ending point for many trails and a great place to refuel after your mountain biking adventure.

Q: Are there any dog-friendly mountain bike routes in Ojén?
A: While specific trails are not explicitly marked as 'dog-friendly' in the data, many natural trails in the Sierra de las Nieves and Sierra Blanca are generally accessible for well-behaved dogs on a leash. It's always advisable to check local regulations for protected areas like the Sierra de las Nieves Natural Park regarding pets.

Q: How long do mountain bike trails in Ojén typically take to complete?
A: Trail durations vary significantly based on distance and difficulty. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e994259296/rio-real-y-sierra-blanca-circular-desde-ojen" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Real y Sierra Blanca – circular desde Ojén&lt;/a&gt;, a 23.6 km difficult trail, typically takes around 2 hours 55 minutes. Longer, more challenging routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33910670" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Río Molinos – Ojén Caves loop from Ojén&lt;/a&gt; can take over 7 hours.

Two beautiful caves located in the heart of Ojén. Admission is free. They are small, but worth seeing. In the past, the caves served as a shelter for people and livestock in bad weather.
